sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 47, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'MOUSE_on_block': 'Toggles individual block between gray and green (N) (VERIFIED steps 3-14)', 'pattern_completion': 'Completing diamond pattern resets grid to all-gray and advances cursor on bar (VERIFIED step 15)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'grid_blocks': '3x3 clickable grid, centers at (50,25),(50,30),(50,35),(55,25),(55,30),(55,35),(60,25),(60,30),(60,35)', 'purple_dots': '4 purple dots in diamond pattern - target pattern', 'bar': 'Horizontal bar with cursor that advances on pattern completion'}
- HUD map: {'right_edge_green': 'Green vertical strip on right edge, likely HUD/timer'}
- Win pattern: {'level_1': '8 stages: complete diamond pattern at each bar position. Each stage requires clicking 4 blocks in diamond formation. Level ends when cursor reaches end of bar.'}
- Level log: {'level_1': '8 stages completed, 45 actions used. Trick: click 4 blocks in diamond pattern at coords (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30) to advance cursor.'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 1 complete. 8 stages done. Cursor reached end of bar, grid blocks turned white indicating game over.
- Goal model: Level complete - no further actions needed.
- Recent findings: ['8 stages total, 4 clicks each = 32 pattern clicks', 'Diamond coords: (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30)']
- Plan: Done - level complete.
- Strategy log: ['Batch MOUSE clicks caused grid reset - use single clicks only']
- Failed probes: ['RIGHT: no change (step 2)', 'MOUSE on cursor area: no change (step 3)', 'batch clicks: caused all-gray reset', 'MOUSE(55,24): wrong position, no toggle', 'MOUSE(55,25): no change (step 17)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 46 actions; a strong score needs about 36 or fewer. You are 1.3x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 44 actions returned the board to a state already seen 44 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 44 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
REVISIT WASTE: this board state has recurred 4 times recently — you are cycling back through states you have already visited. Break the loop and commit to a new line of play.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The RIGHT action had no effect (board_changed: false). The game_over flag is now false again, and state is NOT_FINISHED. But the board looks the same - white blocks, cursor at end of bar. This seems like a terminal state where no more actions can make progress.
Let me check if maybe I need to click on something specific to end the level properly. Looking at the grid, the blocks are white now instead of gray. Maybe I need to click on the cursor position or something else to finalize?
Actually, wait - the game_over was true before but now it's false after RIGHT. Maybe the game isn't actually over, and I need to do something different. Let me look at the current state more carefully.
The grid blocks are white, not gray. The cursor is at the end of the bar. Maybe I need to click on the cursor or the end position to complete the level?
Let me try clicking on the cursor position which appears to be at the right end of the bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Game state: blocks are white, cursor at end of bar. game_over was true but now false.
# Trying clicking the cursor at end of bar to complete level.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 15, 'col': 45}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"MOUSE_on_block": "Toggles individual block between gray and green (N)", "pattern_completion": "Completing diamond pattern resets grid and advances cursor on bar"}, "object_taxonomy": {"grid_blocks": "3x3 clickable grid", "purple_dots": "4 purple dots in diamond pattern - target pattern", "bar": "Horizontal bar with cursor that advances on pattern completion"}, "hud_map": {"right_edge_green": "Green strip on right edge, likely HUD"}, "win_pattern": {"level_1": "8 stages: complete diamond pattern at each bar position. Each stage requires clicking 4 blocks in diamond formation. Level ends when cursor reaches end of bar."}, "level_log": {"level_1": "8 stages completed, 45 actions used. Diamond coords: (50,30), (54,24), (55,35), (60,30)"}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Blocks turned white after stage 8. Cursor at end of bar. Need to find completion action.", "goal_model": "Find action to complete level 1", "recent_findings": ["Blocks are white, not gray. Cursor at end of bar."], "open_questions": ["What action completes the level?"], "current_plan": "Try clicking cursor position", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["RIGHT: no change"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
